message:
So there I was, trying to look up our pediatrician's number during a minor household incident, and—of course—my phone was at 3%. Cue the panic spiral while it's also dying mid-Google search. My partner was asking questions, my kid needed reassurance, and I'm standing there like 'um, does anyone remember our doctor's actual phone number?' 🤦‍♀️

Turns out we couldn't even find it written down anywhere. That's when it hit me: I was *completely* dependent on a device that was literally seconds from becoming a brick.

Now I keep a little printed list tucked in a folder with our important info—doctor, dentist, poison control, the works. Doesn't have to be fancy. Just *there*, waiting for the moment my phone decides to nope out.

Have you ever been caught off guard without a crucial number or bit of info when you really needed it?
